arts.ttl: Chris Sutton insists Man United's defeat to Spurs is the 'type of game that gets managers the SACK' - as the Premier League winner accuses Erik ten Hag of spewing 'verbal diarrhea'
arts.sum: Former Chelsea and Blackburn striker Sutton took aim at the Dutchman's comments, and criticised the players giving fans a lap of honour after the full-time whistle.

arts.ttl: Spurs condemn fans' 'abhorrent' chants at United
arts.sum: Tottenham Hotspur are working with local police and stadium security to identify their supporters who participated in "abhorrent" anti-gay chanting during the 3-0 win over Manchester United at Old Trafford on Sunday.

arts.ttl: Tottenham injury latest: Son Heung-min, Destiny Udogie updates after Man United win
arts.sum: Tottenham Hotspur manager Ange Postecoglou delivers the latest on Destiny Udogie and Son Heung-min's injuries following his side's 3-0 win over Manchester United.

arts.ttl: Erik ten Hag makes remarkably confident statement on his Manchester United future
arts.sum: Manchester United were embarrassed at home by Tottenham Hotspur on Sunday, falling to a thumping 3-0 defeat on home soil with captain Bruno Fernandes sent off

arts.ttl: Erik ten Hag provides double Man United injury update as midfield crisis looms
arts.sum: Erik ten Hag provides a double injury update on Kobbie Mainoo and Mason Mount following Manchester United's 3-0 loss to Tottenham Hotspur in the Premier League.

arts.ttl: Conte explains McTominay strength for Napoli as Manchester United realise mistake
arts.sum: While some wonder whether Manchester United ‘made a mistake’ selling Scott McTominay, Antonio Conte explains...

arts.ttl: Jamie Redknapp names two reasons why he feels sorry for Man Utd boss Erik ten Hag
arts.sum: Erik ten Hag is enduring a miserable time at Manchester United, with the Dutchman left helpless as his side fell to an embarrassing defeat at the hands of Tottenham on Sunday

arts.ttl: Gary Neville tells Man Utd flops to snub Erik ten Hag in crisis meeting
arts.sum: Gary Neville was one of the most vocal critics of Manchester United, calling their performance "disgusting" on commentary - but now feels the players should meet to dissect the painful defeat to Tottenham

arts.ttl: Erik ten Hag explains why Man Utd were destroyed by Tottenham
arts.sum: "We had chances and our moments to get back in the game, with Joshua Zirkzee and Alejandro Garnacho when we still had 11 players and then after the red card too we had moments. Speaking to BBC Sport after Sunday's defeat, United boss Ten Hag criticised the mentality of his players after going a goal down.

arts.ttl: Analysis: How Spurs outplayed Man Utd at Old Trafford
arts.sum: With Maddison often pulling wider to get on the ball early in a move, Spurs’ centre-backs easily passing through a haphazard United press (more on that below), and Destiny Udogie helping to crowd midfield, Kulusevski was able to find intelligent spaces between the lines to carve United open. It will no doubt be a tough week ahead for Erik ten Hag, who has overseen the joint-worst start to a Man Utd season in Premier League history.
